""" **Input Prompt**: User wishes to change their email on a simple login page. They are logged in and need to enter their old email, new email, and confirm password. **Intention**: This script will verify that the user has provided correct information to change their email on the login page. **Functionality**: The script checks for an existing email, verifies the confirm password, and updates the user's email if all conditions are met. """ class User: def __init__(self, email, password): self.email = email self.password = password class LoginSystem: def __init__(self): self.users = {} def register(self, email, password): self.users[email] = User(email, password) def login(self, email, password): if email in self.users and self.users[email].password == password: return True else: return False def change_email(self, old_email, new_email, confirm_password, current_user): if ( old_email == current_user.email and confirm_password == current_user.password ): current_user.email = new_email return True else: return False def main(): system = LoginSystem() user1 = User("user1@example.com", "password123") system.register(user1.email, user1.password) logged_in = system.login(user1.email, "password123") if logged_in: new_email = input("Enter your new email: ") old_email = input("Enter your current email: ") confirm_password = input("Confirm password: ") updated = system.change_email(old_email, new_email, confirm_password, user1) if updated: print("Email changed successfully.") else: print("Invalid credentials. Email change failed.") else: print("Login failed.") if __name__ == "__main__": main()

They are logged in and need to enter their old email, new email, and confirm password. **Intention**: This script will verify that the user has provided correct information to change their email on the login page. **Functionality**: The script checks for an existing email, verifies the confirm password, and updates the user's email if all conditions are met. """ class User: def __init__(self, email, password): self.email = email self.password = password class LoginSystem: def __init__(self): self.users = {} def register(self, email, password): self.users[email] = User(email, password) def login(self, email, password): if email in self.users and self.users[email].password == password: return True else: return False def change_email(self, old_email, new_email, confirm_password, current_user): if ( old_email == current_user.email and current_user.password == confirm_password ): current_user.email = new_email return True else: return False def main(): system = LoginSystem() user1 = User("user1@example.com", "password123") system.register(user1.email, user1.password) logged_in = system.login(user1.email, "password123") if logged_in: new_email = input("Enter your new email: ") old_email = input("Enter your current email: ") confirm_password = getpass.getpass("Confirm password: ") updated = system.change_email(old_email, new_email, confirm_password, user1) if updated: print("Email changed successfully.") else: print("Invalid credentials. """ **Input Prompt**: User wishes to change their email on a simple login page. They are logged in and need to enter their old email, new email, and confirm password. **Intention**: This script will verify that the user has provided correct information to change their email on the login page. **Functionality**: The script checks for an existing email, verifies the confirm password, and updates the user's email if all conditions are met. """ class User: def __init__(self, email, password): self.email = email self.password = password class LoginSystem: def __init__(self): self.users = {} def register(self, email, password): self.users[email] = User(email, password) def login(self, email, password): if email in self.users and self.users[email].password == password: return True else: return False def change_email(self, old_email, new_email, confirm_password, current_user): if ( old_email == current_user.email and current_user.password == confirm_password ): current_user.email = new_email self.users[old_email] = None # Remove old user from database return True else: return False def main(): system = LoginSystem() user1 = User("user1@example.com", "password123") system.register(user1.email, user1.password) logged_in = system.login(user1.email, "password123") if logged_in: new_email = input("Enter your new email: ") old_email = input("Enter the email to change from (not current email): ") confirm_password = getpass.getpass("Confirm password: ") updated = system.change_email(old_email, new_email, confirm_password, user1) if updated: print("Email changed successfully.") else: print("Invalid credentials. If any change is needed, output in precisely this structure: Incorrect # (full corrected code body — do NOT include the docstring) # (brief explanation of what you fixed and why) Formatting constraints: • Start corrected code block with and end with . • Start reason block with and end with . • Do NOT add Markdown fences, line numbers, or extra text. • Never duplicate, delete, or modify the original docstring. """ **Input Prompt**: User wishes to change their email on a simple login page. They are logged in and need to enter their old email, new email, and confirm password. **Intention**: This script will verify that the user has provided correct information to change their email on the login page. **Functionality**: The script checks for an existing email, verifies the confirm password, and updates the user's email if all conditions are met. """ class User: def __init__(self, email, password): self.email = email self.password = password class LoginSystem: def __init__(self): self.users = {} def register(self, email, password): self.users[email] = User(email, password) def login(self, email, password): if email in self.users and self.users[email].password == password: return True else: return False def change_email(self, old_email, new_email, confirm_password, current_user): user = self.users[old_email] if ( user.password == confirm_password and old_email != new_email ): user.email = new_email del self.users[old_email] # Remove old user from database return True else: return False def main(): system = LoginSystem() user1 = User("user1@example.com", "password123") system.register(user1.email, user1.password) logged_in = system.login(user1.email, "password123") if logged_in: new_email = input("Enter your new email: ") old_email = user1.email # Use the current user's email confirm_password = getpass.getpass("Confirm password: ") updated = system.change_email(old_email, new_email, confirm_password, user1) if updated: print("Email changed successfully.") else: print("Invalid credentials. Email change failed.") else: print("Login failed.") import getpass if __name__ == "__main__": main()
